---
title: "Other Methods"
---

# Scope

This page groups the remaining core quasi-experimental methods in the notes section. Use it when matching is not the main candidate design and the real question is whether the evaluation is better framed around time variation, a threshold, or a cross-method comparison.

# Main Routes

## [Difference-in-Differences](difference-in-differences.qmd)

Use this when treated and untreated units are both observed over time and the design depends on a credible parallel-trends argument.

## [Regression Discontinuity](regression-discontinuity.qmd)

Use this when treatment assignment is governed by a rule or threshold and units close to the cutoff can support a local comparison.

## [Comparing The Main Methods](comparing-methods.qmd)

Use this page when several designs are still plausible and you need to compare them on assumptions, data structure, diagnostics, and limits.

## [Choosing A Method](choosing-a-method.qmd)

Use this page when the practical task is to move from a policy problem and observed data structure to a defensible design choice.

## [Mixtape Sessions: Twitch Training Delivery](mixtape-sessions-twitch-training-notes.qmd)

Use this page when the goal is to study how Scott Cunningham structures, delivers, and extends a causal-inference workshop, and to translate those training patterns into QED Labs practice.

## [Mixtape Sessions: Twitch Workshop Content by Day](mixtape-sessions-twitch-day-by-day-notes.qmd)

Use this page when the goal is to follow the content sequence of the four-day Twitch workshop closely and review what Cunningham covers each day.

# Working Rule

The method should be chosen by the assignment process and the data-generating structure, not by which estimator is most familiar or easiest to run.
